住吉神社 | image = Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Honden.JPG | caption = Honden of Sumiyoshi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|59|58.6|N|130|57|23.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Sumiyoshi | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 11-1 Ichinomiyasumiyoshi 1-chōme, Shimonoseki-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 751-0805 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = December 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the Miyasumiyoshi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]] in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on December 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Along with the more famous [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] and the [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi Jinja]] in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], it is one of the "Three Great Sumiyoshi" shrines; however whereas the Osaka Sumitomo-taisha enshrines the ''[[:en:Mitama|Nigi-Mitama]]'', or placid spirit of the Sumiyoshi ''[[kami]]'', the shrine in Shimonoseki enshrines the ''Ara-Mitama'', or rough spirit of the ''kami''.{{cite encyclopedia|last=Yonei|first=Teruyoshi|title=Aramitama|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1180|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Kokugakuin University|access-date=5 February 2011}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Sumiyoshi Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]'''| 住吉三神|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]'''| 応神天皇|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''| 武内宿禰命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'''| 神功皇后|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takeminakata]]'''|建御名方命|}} ==History== The origins of Itakiso Jinja are unknown. Per the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] embarked on her conquest of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], she entrusted the Sumitomo sanjin to protect her passage across the ocean. En route back to Japan, she had a message from the gods that their oracle was to be found in Nagato Province, where a shrine should be built. The shrine first appears in the historical record in an entry dated 859 in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. From the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it received donations from successive [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]], including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku]] and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]], ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Shimonoseki Station|Shin-Shimonoseki Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', [[:en:Silla|Silla]], with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] }} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Shimonoseki Station|Shin-Shimonoseki Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', [[:en:Silla|Silla]], with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] }} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

From the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it received donations from successive [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]], including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku]] and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]], ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Shimonoseki Station|Shin-Shimonoseki Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', [[:en:Silla|Silla]], with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381858]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine
住吉神社 | image = Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Honden.JPG | caption = Honden of Sumiyoshi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|59|58.6|N|130|57|23.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Sumiyoshi | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 11-1 Ichinomiyasumiyoshi 1-chōme, Shimonoseki-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 751-0805 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = December 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the Miyasumiyoshi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]] in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on December 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Along with the more famous [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] and the [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi Jinja]] in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], it is one of the "Three Great Sumiyoshi" shrines; however whereas the Osaka Sumitomo-taisha enshrines the ''[[:en:Mitama|Nigi-Mitama]]'', or placid spirit of the Sumiyoshi ''[[kami]]'', the shrine in Shimonoseki enshrines the ''Ara-Mitama'', or rough spirit of the ''kami''.{{cite encyclopedia|last=Yonei|first=Teruyoshi|title=Aramitama|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1180|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Kokugakuin University|access-date=5 February 2011}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Sumiyoshi Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]'''| 住吉三神|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]'''| 応神天皇|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''| 武内宿禰命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'''| 神功皇后|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takeminakata]]'''|建御名方命|}} ==History== The origins of Itakiso Jinja are unknown. Per the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] embarked on her conquest of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], she entrusted the Sumitomo sanjin to protect her passage across the ocean. En route back to Japan, she had a message from the gods that their oracle was to be found in Nagato Province, where a shrine should be built. The shrine first appears in the historical record in an entry dated 859 in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. From the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it received donations from successive [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]], including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku]] and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]], ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Shimonoseki Station|Shin-Shimonoseki Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', [[:en:Silla|Silla]], with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381858]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine
住吉神社 | image = Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Honden.JPG | caption = Honden of Sumiyoshi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|59|58.6|N|130|57|23.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Sumiyoshi | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 11-1 Ichinomiyasumiyoshi 1-chōme, Shimonoseki-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 751-0805 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = December 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the Miyasumiyoshi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]] in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on December 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Along with the more famous [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] and the [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi Jinja]] in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], it is one of the "Three Great Sumiyoshi" shrines; however whereas the Osaka Sumitomo-taisha enshrines the ''[[:en:Mitama|Nigi-Mitama]]'', or placid spirit of the Sumiyoshi ''[[kami]]'', the shrine in Shimonoseki enshrines the ''Ara-Mitama'', or rough spirit of the ''kami''.{{cite encyclopedia|last=Yonei|first=Teruyoshi|title=Aramitama|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1180|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Kokugakuin University|access-date=5 February 2011}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Sumiyoshi Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]'''| 住吉三神|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]'''| 応神天皇|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''| 武内宿禰命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'''| 神功皇后|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takeminakata]]'''|建御名方命|}} ==History== The origins of Itakiso Jinja are unknown. Per the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] embarked on her conquest of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], she entrusted the Sumitomo sanjin to protect her passage across the ocean. En route back to Japan, she had a message from the gods that their oracle was to be found in Nagato Province, where a shrine should be built. The shrine first appears in the historical record in an entry dated 859 in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. From the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it received donations from successive [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]], including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku]] and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]], ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Shimonoseki Station|Shin-Shimonoseki Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', [[:en:Silla|Silla]], with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. [[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381858]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine
住吉神社 | image = Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Honden.JPG | caption = Honden of Sumiyoshi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|59|58.6|N|130|57|23.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Sumiyoshi | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 11-1 Ichinomiyasumiyoshi 1-chōme, Shimonoseki-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 751-0805 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = December 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|[[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the Miyasumiyoshi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]] in [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on December 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Along with the more famous [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] and the [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi Jinja]] in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], it is one of the "Three Great Sumiyoshi" shrines; however whereas the Osaka Sumitomo-taisha enshrines the ''[[:en:Mitama|Nigi-Mitama]]'', or placid spirit of the Sumiyoshi ''[[kami]]'', the shrine in Shimonoseki enshrines the ''Ara-Mitama'', or rough spirit of the ''kami''.{{cite encyclopedia|last=Yonei|first=Teruyoshi|title=Aramitama|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1180|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Kokugakuin University|access-date=5 February 2011}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Sumiyoshi Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]'''| 住吉三神|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]'''| 応神天皇|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''| 武内宿禰命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]'''| 神功皇后|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takeminakata]]'''|建御名方命|}} ==History== The origins of Itakiso Jinja are unknown. Per the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] embarked on her conquest of the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], she entrusted the Sumitomo sanjin to protect her passage across the ocean. En route back to Japan, she had a message from the gods that their oracle was to be found in Nagato Province, where a shrine should be built. The shrine first appears in the historical record in an entry dated 859 in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. From the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it received donations from successive [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]], including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku]] and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and the [[:en:Mōri clan|Mōri clan]], ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Chōshū Domain|Chōshū Domain]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Shimonoseki Station|Shin-Shimonoseki Station]] on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', [[:en:Silla|Silla]], with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]], used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. [[:en:Sōgi|Sōgi]], a leading figure in ''[[:en:Renga|Renga]]'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of [[:en:Ōuchi Masahiro|Ōuchi Masahiro]], where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]], and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

住吉神社 | image = Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Honden.JPG | caption = Honden of Sumiyoshi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|59|58.6|N|130|57|23.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Sumiyoshi | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 11-1 Ichinomiyasumiyoshi 1-chōme, Shimonoseki-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 751-0805 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = December 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the Miyasumiyoshi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]] in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}}. Per the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} embarked on her conquest of the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, she entrusted the Sumitomo sanjin to protect her passage across the ocean. En route back to Japan, she had a message from the gods that their oracle was to be found in Nagato Province, where a shrine should be built. The shrine first appears in the historical record in an entry dated 859 in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. From the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, it received donations from successive {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}, including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku}} and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}} and the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan}}, ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}}. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Shimonoseki Station|en|Shin-Shimonoseki Station|WD=Q1074353|lt=Shin-Shimonoseki Station}} on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. {{ill|Sōgi|en|Sōgi|WD=Q923562|lt=Sōgi}}, a leading figure in ''{{ill|Renga|en|Renga|WD=Q1506883|lt=Renga}}'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of {{ill|Ōuchi Masahiro|en|Ōuchi Masahiro|WD=Q8081786|lt=Ōuchi Masahiro}}, where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|en|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|WD=Q349452|lt=Emperor Go-Kashiwabara}}, and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]][[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381858]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine
住吉神社 | image = Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Honden.JPG | caption = Honden of Sumiyoshi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|59|58.6|N|130|57|23.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Sumiyoshi | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 11-1 Ichinomiyasumiyoshi 1-chōme, Shimonoseki-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 751-0805 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = December 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=red|text align=center|{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}}} }} {{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the Miyasumiyoshi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]] in {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on December 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} Along with the more famous [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}} and the [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi Jinja]] in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}}, it is one of the "Three Great Sumiyoshi" shrines; however whereas the Osaka Sumitomo-taisha enshrines the ''{{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Nigi-Mitama}}'', or placid spirit of the Sumiyoshi ''[[kami]]'', the shrine in Shimonoseki enshrines the ''Ara-Mitama'', or rough spirit of the ''kami''.{{cite encyclopedia|last=Yonei|first=Teruyoshi|title=Aramitama|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1180|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Kokugakuin University|access-date=5 February 2011}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Sumiyoshi Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]'''| 住吉三神|}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}'''| 応神天皇|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''| 武内宿禰命 |}} * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}'''| 神功皇后|}} * {{nihongo|'''[[Takeminakata]]'''|建御名方命|}} ==History== The origins of Itakiso Jinja are unknown. Per the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} embarked on her conquest of the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, she entrusted the Sumitomo sanjin to protect her passage across the ocean. En route back to Japan, she had a message from the gods that their oracle was to be found in Nagato Province, where a shrine should be built. The shrine first appears in the historical record in an entry dated 859 in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''. In the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' it is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province. The shrine was worshipped by the military classes and as a guardian of maritime traffic. From the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, it received donations from successive {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}, including [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. Although the shrine declined in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku}} and [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]s it was patronized by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}} and the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan}}, ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Chōshū Domain|en|Chōshū Domain|WD=Q876267|lt=Chōshū Domain}}. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} in 1871, and promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1911.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a twenty-minute walk from {{ill|Shin-Shimonoseki Station|en|Shin-Shimonoseki Station|WD=Q1074353|lt=Shin-Shimonoseki Station}} on the [[:en:Sanyo Shinkansen|Sanyo Shinkansen]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Cultural properties== ===National Treasures=== *'''[[Honden]]''', built in 1370. This a unique structure based on a modification of the ''[[nagare-zukuri]]'' style, but with five separate bays, each with its own small gable, to provide five sanctuaries, one for each of the five ''kami'' worshipped at the shrine. It interior is decorated with colored paintings.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3199|title=住吉神社本殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]''', constructed in 1539. It is a three by one bay structure with a single-gable roof which is covered in cypress bark shingles. This hall of worship is built at right angles to the center of the front of the main hall. The floor is low and open on all sides. Detailed techniques of its construction are characteristic of the end of the Muromachi period.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3200|title=住吉神社拝殿|trans-title=Sumiyoshi Jinja Haiden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Bronze Bell''', {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, with an overall height of 147.0 cm and a diameter of 78.3 cm is the largest Korean bell in Japan. The body is long and smooth, with a bulge on the waist, and a slightly squeezed rim. The body has lotus petal and cloud decorations with four celestial maidens flying in the air.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5468|title=銅鐘|trans-title=Dosho|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''Horse fittings''', {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}. These include a saddle, harness and stirrups with an old bronze peony arabesque decoration, in the style of the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, used for festivals and ceremonies. The set was a donation by [[:en:Ouchi Yoshitaka|Ouchi Yoshitaka]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/56868|title=金銅牡丹唐草透唐鞍|trans-title=Gold bronze arabesque saddle|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} *'''''Horaku Hyakushu Waka'' Strip''', dated 1495. {{ill|Sōgi|en|Sōgi|WD=Q923562|lt=Sōgi}}, a leading figure in ''{{ill|Renga|en|Renga|WD=Q1506883|lt=Renga}}'' poetry, came to Nagato twice (1480 and 1489) under the protection of {{ill|Ōuchi Masahiro|en|Ōuchi Masahiro|WD=Q8081786|lt=Ōuchi Masahiro}}, where he played a major role in editing the ''Shinsen Tsukubashū''. He donated a strip of poetry with 30 works by famous people, including Sanjōnishi Sanetaka, {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|en|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|WD=Q349452|lt=Emperor Go-Kashiwabara}}, and others in a cedar box, It was later divided into individual works and placed in a lacquer box by [[Mōri Hidemoto]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/8265 |title=住吉社法楽百首和歌短冊（明応四年十二月）|trans-title=Sumiyoshi-sha Horaku Hyakushu Waka Strip|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Romon gate and sando of Sumiyoshi Shrine.jpg|Romon Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki) Haiden.JPG|Haiden 1st torii of Sumiyoshi Shrine 3.jpg|1st Torii ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|WD=Q3245520|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_10469.htmlYamaguchi Prefecture Tourist Information] ]]]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} [[ja:住吉神社 (下関市)]] [[zh:住吉神社 (下關市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381858}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Nagato Province]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]
